events.text: Steven Lopez, 52-year-old White male, Never smoker. Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.

Diagnosed with Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 of the Right kidney. Clinical staging: T3aN0M1, AJCC Stage IV. ICD-10: C64.9. Stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma with lung metastases.
Bone scan performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Underwent cytoreductive nephrectomy: Right radical nephrectomy with adrenalectomy. Estimated blood loss 300 mL. Length of stay 6 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Axitinib 5 mg BID. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Axitinib 5 mg BID. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 1 proteinuria. 24-hour urine protein 1.2 g. Monitoring continued. No dose modification needed.
Initiated first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Axitinib 5 mg BID. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 immune-related thyroiditis. TSH elevated to 12.5. Levothyroxine 50 mcg daily started.
Initiated first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w + Axitinib 5 mg BID. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination.
Clinical visit for results review after first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination.
Initiated second-line immunotherapy-TKI: Nivolumab 240 mg q2w + Cabozantinib 40 mg daily. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for follow-up on second-line immunotherapy-TKI.
Clinical visit for follow-up on second-line immunotherapy-TKI.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging on second-line immunotherapy-TKI.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
MRI abdomen with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
BONE SCAN REPORT

Patient: Steven Lopez
MRN: patient_6e7978fd0327
Date of Study: 2023-12-18

CLINICAL INDICATION: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Whole body bone scintigraphy performed following IV injection of 25 mCi Tc-99m MDP.

FINDINGS:
No scintigraphic evidence of osseous metastatic disease. Degenerative uptake in bilateral knees.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right renal mass consistent with renal cell carcinoma. Pulmonary metastases.

Electronically signed by: Jennifer Walsh, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Steven Lopez
MRN: patient_6e7978fd0327
Specimen ID: S2023-33276
Date of Procedure: 2023-12-20
Date of Report: 2023-12-24

SPECIMEN: Right kidney, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 2.9 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Present

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
CA-IX: Positive (diffuse membranous)
CD10: Positive
CK7: Negative
Vimentin: Positive

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Steven Lopez - Right kidney" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 2.9 x 2.0 x 1.4 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm yellow-tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A10.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of nests of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows minimal reactive changes.

Electronically signed by: Emily Nakamura, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Steven Lopez
MRN: patient_6e7978fd0327
Date: 2023-12-25
Provider: Dr. Richard Okafor

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Lopez is a 52-year-old male who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney, IV. Diagnosis made following evaluation of progressive symptoms. Stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma with lung metastases. Pathology confirmed clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3. Molecular testing is pending. Patient reports progressive dyspnea and cough x 6 weeks.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- obstructive sleep apnea
- osteoarthritis

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Right knee arthroscopy (age 45)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Former smoker (quit 5 years ago)
- Alcohol: Social drinker (2-3 drinks/week)
- Occupation: Accountant
- Lives with spouse

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Sister with ovarian cancer at age 55.

ALLERGIES: Sulfa drugs (hives)

MEDICATIONS:
- None

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: joint pain, dry mouth, mild cough, mild peripheral edema. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 147/75, HR 73, RR 17, Temp 98.1F, SpO2 98% on RA, Wt 70.6 kg
General: Alert, oriented, well-appearing, in no acute distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 3.4, Hgb 12.9, Plt 286, Cr 1.19

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
52-year-old male with IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney. initial oncology consultation.

1. Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney, Stage IV
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend cytoreductive nephrectomy
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - PICC line to be placed prior to treatment
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Amanda Foster, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Steven Lopez
MRN: patient_6e7978fd0327
Date of Report: 2024-01-05
Specimen: Right kidney (Resection)
Diagnosis: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3
Panel: Guardant360 CDx (648 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 2.3 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  VHL p.L89P (c.266T>C) - Missense (Exon 1) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  PBRM1 p.Q1298* - Nonsense (Exon 24)
  BAP1 Loss - Deletion
  SETD2 p.R1740* - Nonsense (Exon 11)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  KDM5C p.A388T - VUS
  MTOR p.S2215Y - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - VHL mutation: Consistent with clear cell histology. HIF-pathway targeted therapies (TKIs) and immunotherapy combinations recommended.
  - BAP1 loss: Associated with more aggressive biology.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 430x coverage.
Tumor content: 65%.

Electronically signed by: Katherine Liu, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Steven Lopez
MRN: patient_6e7978fd0327
Specimen ID: S2024-86580
Date of Procedure: 2024-01-22
Date of Report: 2024-01-24

SPECIMEN: Right kidney, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 8.0 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Present

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
PAX8: Positive
CA-IX: Positive (diffuse membranous)
CD10: Positive
CK7: Negative
Vimentin: Positive

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Steven Lopez - Right kidney" is a tan-pink tissue fragment measuring 8.0 x 5.6 x 4.0 cm. The cut surface reveals a soft yellow-tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A10.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are occasionally seen (3 per 10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: Michael Torres, MD
Department of Pathology

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Medical Oncology

Patient: Steven Lopez
MRN: patient_6e7978fd0327
Date: 2024-01-30
Provider: Dr. Amanda Foster

CHIEF COMPLAINT: postoperative follow-up

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Lopez is a 52-year-old male with IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney who presents for postoperative follow-up. Patient is tolerating treatment reasonably well. Appetite has improved.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: mild peripheral edema, constipation, joint pain. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 120/85, HR 93, RR 19, Temp 98.6F, SpO2 97% on RA, Wt 74.9 kg
General: Alert, oriented, well-appearing, in no acute distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 8.6, Hgb 10.6, Plt 217, Cr 1.15

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
52-year-old male with IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma, fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney. postoperative follow-up.

1. Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 - postoperative follow-up
   - Treatment well tolerated, continue
   - Monitoring for treatment-related toxicity
2. Symptom management
   - Pain well controlled on current regimen
3. Follow-up in 2 weeks

Electronically signed by: Amanda Foster, MD

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Steven Lopez
MRN: patient_6e7978fd0327
Date of Study: 2024-05-28

CLINICAL INDICATION: Clear cell renal cell carcinoma, Fuhrman grade 3 of the right kidney. restaging after first-line immunotherapy-TKI combination.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Few pulmonary nodules, largest measuring 1.7 cm in the right lower lobe. Consistent with metastatic disease.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Heterogeneously enhancing mass arising from the right kidney measuring 8.2 x 6.6 cm confined to the renal parenchyma.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Right renal mass consistent with renal cell carcinoma. Pulmonary metastases.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology
